key_insight: An OMOP-standardized prostate cancer database plus NLP and PSA-based algorithms greatly improved capture of key clinical details from EHR data.
clinical_relevance: This approach can make real-world prostate cancer datasets more complete and reliable for research, quality checks, and registry support.
method: Converted UTMB Epic EHR data (2010-2021) into OMOP CDM v5.4.
Used NLP to extract PSA, Gleason score, and cancer stage from clinical notes.
Applied PSA-based algorithms to identify missing treatment information and biochemical recurrence.
evidence_signal: Of 815 analytic cases, 700 (85.9%) were complete and concordant with the cancer registry; PSA agreement was excellent, and NLP materially improved sparse structured Gleason and stage data.
watch_item: Performance depends on local data quality and charting patterns, so NLP and rule-based algorithms may need site-specific validation before broader use.
paper_title: Developing an OMOP-Standardized Prostate Cancer Database and Improving Data Quality Using NLP and PSA-Based Algorithms
